opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I spilled a pot of boiling water down my leg about an hour ago, the pain is absolutely blinding, and these massive blisters are blowing up. My wife is here with me. What do I do?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden very painful scald to the thigh from boiling water with big blisters coming up fast — wants pain relief and to know how to look after it.

symptom_narrative.onset: about an hour ago, right after a pot of boiling water tipped down the front of my leg
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly one hour and the blisters are still coming up
symptom_narrative.character: a fierce, continuous burning; the skin is bright red and there are big fluid-filled blisters
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: absolutely blinding, the worst pain I can remember — even the air moving over it makes me flinch
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: front of my right thigh, a patch about the size of my palm
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it happened, spikes with any movement or a draught over it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: my wife ran cool water over it for about 20 minutes straight away and that helped a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any movement of the leg or air blowing across the raw skin
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no chest symptoms or feeling faint; just the pain and the blisters
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't really move the leg or stand on it because of the pain

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home, spouse present and helping; kitchen spill, no one else hurt

objective_data_offered.value: on video the front of the right thigh shows a patch of bright red skin roughly 8 by 10 cm with three large intact fluid-filled blisters over it; the surrounding skin looks normal
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: appearance on a phone camera only; depth and true surface area cannot be judged reliably from video

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says the blisters are thin-walled and filling with clear fluid, the red area does not look white or leathery, and he is sweaty and wincing but breathing normally
cooled it under cool running tap water for about 20 minutes right after it happened; nothing else applied, no creams or ice
about 82 kg on the home scales
